title: Delicate Roast German Pork Roast
description: A comforting German dish featuring elderflower and roast pork, perfect for a cold evening. This recipe is free from onions and garlic, making it suitable for those with sensitivities.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, whisk together the elderflower syrup, apple cider vinegar, honey, caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper.
Place the pork roast in a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot.
Brush the elderflower mixture evenly over the pork roast, making sure to coat all surfaces.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the pork for 20 minutes per pound, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the pork rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
Serve with your choice of sides, such as braised red cabbage or roasted root vegetables.
